questions.q: Who was Yuyutsu's father?
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu was a son of King Dhritarashtra, half-brother to the hundred Kauravas.

questions.q: How did Yuyutsu differ in birth from Duryodhana and his brothers?
options: He was born of a Vaishya attendant, not Queen Gandhari
He was born of a rishi's blessing
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu was Dhritarashtra's son by a Vaishya serving-woman who attended Gandhari.

questions.q: What momentous choice did Yuyutsu make as the Kurukshetra War began?
options: He crossed over to fight for the Pandavas
He renounced arms and left for the forest
He took command of the Kaurava rearguard
questions.explanation: When Yudhishthira invited any warrior to change sides, Yuyutsu alone walked across to the Pandavas.

questions.q: Who made the open offer that allowed warriors to switch sides before battle?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira, facing the armies, declared that anyone might choose his side before the first arrow.

questions.q: Did Yuyutsu survive the Kurukshetra War?
options: Yes — he was among its few survivors
No — he fell on the last day
No — Bhima slew him by mistake
He vanished during the night raid
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu survived, one of the handful of warriors left alive from the Kaurava line.

questions.q: What does the name 'Yuyutsu' mean?
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu means 'one desirous of battle', from the root yudh, to fight.

questions.q: How many sons of Dhritarashtra survived the war?
questions.explanation: All hundred of Gandhari's sons perished; only Yuyutsu, the Vaishya-born son, lived on.

questions.q: Which side's funeral rites did Yuyutsu help perform after the war?
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu joined the offerings for Duryodhana and his brothers, the kin he had opposed in war.

questions.q: To which great dynasty did Yuyutsu belong?
questions.explanation: As Dhritarashtra's son, Yuyutsu was a prince of the Kuru house of Hastinapura.

questions.q: Roughly how many warriors switched sides when Yudhishthira's offer was made?
questions.explanation: Of the millions assembled, the epic records just one man crossing over: Yuyutsu.

questions.q: Why is Yuyutsu's defection treated as an act of dharma rather than betrayal?
options: He chose righteousness over a doomed, adharmic cause
He had been raised by the Pandavas
Krishna had promised him a kingdom
questions.explanation: The epic frames his crossing as conscience triumphing over kin-loyalty to an unjust side.

questions.q: In popular retellings, what response follows Yuyutsu as he crosses the field?
options: The Pandavas mocked his Vaishya birth
Duryodhana and his brothers jeered at him
The soldiers pelted him with dust
questions.explanation: Later retellings describe Duryodhana and his brothers jeering as Yuyutsu walked over; the epic itself records the crossing tersely, without dwelling on taunts.

questions.q: During Gandhari's long, troubled pregnancy, what marks Yuyutsu's birth in the chronology?
options: He was born years after Duryodhana
He was born the same span as Gandhari's hundred sons, by the attendant who served her
He was the eldest of all Dhritarashtra's children
He was born after the war began
questions.explanation: While Gandhari's two-year pregnancy ran its strange course, the Vaishya attendant bore Dhritarashtra this son, of an age with Duryodhana.

questions.q: What position did Yudhishthira grant Yuyutsu after the war?
options: A place of trust overseeing affairs for the old king and later the realm
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira honored him with stewardship duties, serving Dhritarashtra's household and the kingdom's care.

questions.q: When the Pandavas departed on their final journey, what charge was given to Yuyutsu?
options: Care of the kingdom as guardian counselor for young Parikshit
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira entrusted the realm's oversight to Yuyutsu as elder guardian, with Parikshit enthroned and Kripa as preceptor.

questions.q: In several tellings, which plot against Bhima did Yuyutsu warn the Pandavas about in their youth?
options: The burning of the lac house
Duryodhana's poisoning of Bhima's food at the river sport
questions.explanation: Tradition credits Yuyutsu with alerting the Pandavas to the poisoned feast at Pramanakoti, though tellings vary.

questions.q: Which Kaurava brother — often confused with Yuyutsu — protested Draupadi's humiliation at the dice game?
questions.explanation: It was Vikarna, Gandhari's son, who spoke against the disrobing; Yuyutsu's famous dissent came at the war's threshold.

questions.q: How did Yuyutsu spend the war after joining the Pandavas?
options: He guarded the supply camp only
He fought as a warrior on the Pandava side through the eighteen days
He sat out the fighting as a counselor
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu fought for the Pandavas and is counted among the war's eleven principal survivors overall.

questions.q: What grief did Yuyutsu carry uniquely after the eighteenth day?
options: He had slain his own teacher
All hundred brothers of his father's house were dead
He alone had seen Krishna's cosmic form
questions.explanation: Victory left him the last son of Dhritarashtra, mourning the very brothers he had opposed.

questions.q: When Dhritarashtra, Gandhari and Kunti retired to the forest, what role fell to Yuyutsu?
options: He remained at court, a bridge between the old king's memory and the new order
questions.explanation: Yuyutsu stayed in royal service at Hastinapura while the elders departed for their forest retreat.

questions.q: What does Yuyutsu's story say about birth and worth in the epic's moral vision?
options: That a serving-woman's son could outshine a hundred queen-born princes in dharma
That mixed birth bars kingship forever
That loyalty to kin overrides all
questions.explanation: The epic pointedly lets the humble-born son be the one righteous survivor of Dhritarashtra's line.

questions.q: In the traditional reckoning of survivors, Yuyutsu is listed alongside which others?
options: The five Pandavas, Krishna, Satyaki, Ashwatthama, Kripa, Kritavarma
questions.explanation: Eleven principal warriors outlived the war — the Pandava five with Krishna and Satyaki, plus three Kaurava-side men and Yuyutsu.

questions.q: By traditional naming, Yuyutsu's mother is sometimes called what in later sources?
questions.explanation: Later texts name the Vaishya attendant Sauvali (or Sughada); Vyasa's epic leaves her unnamed.

questions.q: Because of his mixed kshatriya-vaishya parentage, what class does the epic's genealogy assign to Yuyutsu?
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva itself styles Yuyutsu a karana, the name it gives the son of a kshatriya father and a vaishya mother — distinct from the suta status of Karna's foster line.

questions.q: What, in substance, was the open declaration Yudhishthira made before the armies, which Yuyutsu answered?
options: That fate spares no one who wavers
That kings must die where they stand
That whoever chose to come over to the Pandavas would be accepted as their own
That the gods watch all desertions
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira proclaimed that anyone wishing to choose the Pandavas' side might cross over before battle began, and Yuyutsu alone accepted.

questions.q: What advantage did Yuyutsu's defection give the Pandavas beyond one more warrior?
options: He brought an akshauhini with him
He carried knowledge of Kaurava counsels and, by surviving, secured the Kuru line's continuity of witness
He delivered Duryodhana's battle plans in writing
He opened Hastinapura's gates that night
questions.explanation: An insider of Duryodhana's court and a surviving son of Dhritarashtra, he became both witness and bridge after the slaughter.

questions.q: How does Dhritarashtra regard Yuyutsu in the war's aftermath, as the epic portrays?
options: He disowns him as a traitor
He accepts him — the one son left to light the ancestral offerings
He refuses ever to see him
He crowns him king over Parikshit
questions.explanation: Bitter as the old king's grief is, Yuyutsu remains his single living son and a comfort of the house.

questions.q: Which parva narrates Yuyutsu's crossing to the Pandava side?
questions.explanation: The crossing happens at the war's very brink, told in the Bhishma Parva before the first day's fighting.

questions.q: During the war, which Kaurava-side warrior is recorded getting the better of Yuyutsu in a duel of arrows?
questions.explanation: The Karna Parva records Uluka, Shakuni's son, worsting Yuyutsu in an exchange of arrows and driving him from the fight.

questions.q: What quiet irony crowns Yuyutsu's arc when Parikshit takes the throne?
options: The despised attendant's son ends as elder statesman of the very dynasty whose heirs scorned him
He becomes a forest hermit after all
He is finally crowned king of Gandhara
questions.explanation: The survivor of low birth stands guardian over the Kuru future — a deliberate inversion of Duryodhana's pride.
